Since this is a code generation tool in /eng/packages/, it's actually a CodeGen issue, not a management SDK issue itself. it's the management generator, so "Mgmt" seems like the right category even though it's in `/eng/packages/`. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.","type":"string"},"direction":{"description":"Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.","enum":["ASC","DESC"],"type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Filter by labels","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"orderBy":{"description":"Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}

2026-04-09T01:45:45.4804669Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T01:43:41.861836063Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"57967","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-09T01:45:45.4838662Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T01:43:42.193320016Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57967,\"title\":\"[Azure Management Generator] Docs: Document scope-based extension resource parameter ordering and complex parent path ValidateRe\\n[Content truncated due to length]\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57934 by `@live1206`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nTwo bug fixes were merged for the Azure Management SDK generator/emitter:\\n\\n1. **Generator (`OperationContext.cs`):** Fixed a LIFO stack push-order bug in `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy`. For scope-based extension resources (e.g., `ChaosTarget`, `KubernetesClusterExtension`), the stack was producing reversed positional order, causing `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` to be swapped in REST call arguments.\\n\\n2. **Emitter (`resource-metadata.ts`):** Fixed `getExpectedParentResourceType` to handle parent paths with more than 3 segments. For resources scoped under nested multi-provider paths (e.g., `managementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/...`), the function now falls back to computing the parent scope resource type from the last `/providers/` segment, enabling `ValidateResourceId` generation for those collections.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `Extension Resources` section in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` (lines 162–168) is a two-sentence placeholder. It does not explain scope-based extension resources, how the `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy` LIFO stack resolves positional parameters for them, or the interaction between `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` for extension resource contexts.\\n- There is no documentation describing how the emitter\\u0026#39;s `getExpectedParentResourceType` function computes the expected parent resource type for collection resources, the 3-segment rule, or the fallback to the last `/providers/` segment for multi-provider nested scopes.\\n- There is no changelog entry for either fix in the `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` package — there is no `CHANGELOG.md` at all in that directory.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` (edit)\\n\\nExpand the **Extension Resources** section (currently lines ~162–168) to cover scope-based extension resources in detail. Replace the current terse section with:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Extension Resources\\n\\nExtension resources have a flexible scope — they can be attached to any Azure resource. Their contextual path starts with a single `resourceUri` variable:\\n\\n```\\n/{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName}\\n```\\n\\nThe `resourceUri` parameter is treated as a contextual parameter derived from the parent resource\\u0026#39;s `Id`.\\n\\n#### Scope-Based Extension Resources\\n\\nSome extension resources also capture the parent resource **type** as a contextual parameter in addition to its name. For example, a `ChaosTarget` contextual path looks like:\\n\\n```\\n/{scope}/providers/Microsoft.Chaos/targets/{targetName}\\n````\\n\\nWhen the generator builds `ContextualParameter` objects for such resources, each variable segment in the path is paired with the constant key that precedes it using a LIFO (stack-based) algorithm. The variable pairs are pushed onto the stack in **key-then-value** order so that the resulting positional list matches the path order from outermost to innermost scope.\\n\\nThe generated REST call correctly supplies arguments in this order:\\n\\n````csharp\\n_restClient.Get(\\n    Id.Parent.ResourceType.Type,  // e.g., \\u0026#34;Microsoft.Kubernetes/connectedClusters\\u0026#34;\\n    Id.Parent.Name,               // e.g., \\u0026#34;myCluster\\u0026#34;\\n    Id.Name,                      // e.g., \\u0026#34;myTarget\\u0026#34;\\n    cancellationToken\\n);\\n```\\n\\nIf the stack push order were reversed (a previous bug), `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` would be swapped, producing incorrect REST calls.\\n````\\n\\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` (edit)\\n\\nAdd a new subsection under **Advanced Scenarios** documenting the emitter\\u0026#39;s `ValidateResourceId` generation for complex parent paths. Add after the **Singleton Resources** subsection:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### ValidateResourceId for Complex Multi-Provider Scopes\\n\\nWhen generating a resource collection, the emitter computes the **expected parent resource type** to emit a `ValidateResourceId` guard in the `GetAll` method. For simple paths (≤ 3 segments), the parent type is derived directly from the second-to-last provider segment.\\n\\nFor nested multi-provider paths with more than 3 segments — such as:\\n\\n```\\nmanagementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/quotas\\n```\\n\\nthe emitter falls back to computing the parent scope resource type from the **last `/providers/`** segment in the path. This ensures that `ValidateResourceId` is emitted correctly for these collection types even when the parent path has complex, multi-level scope nesting.\\n````\\n\\n#### File 3: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/CHANGELOG.md` (create)\\n\\nCreate a new CHANGELOG file to track generator/emitter changes:\\n\\n```markdown\\n# Changelog\\n\\n## Unreleased\\n\\n### Bug Fixes\\n\\n- Fixed incorrect parameter ordering for scope-based extension resources (e.g., `ChaosTarget`, `KubernetesClusterExtension`). The LIFO stack in `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy` was reversed, swapping `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` in generated REST client arguments.\\n- Fixed `ValidateResourceId` generation for resource collections whose parent paths contain more than 3 segments (nested multi-provider scopes such as `managementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/...`). The emitter now falls back to the last `/providers/` segment to compute the expected parent resource type.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo README snippet placeholders are needed for these documentation-only changes (the docs are in `.md` files under `docs/`, not in a package README that uses the snippet generation tooling).\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is an npm package README (not a client library README), so the standard section checklist (Getting Started, Key Concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, etc.) is not required. No changes needed to `README.md`.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nThe docs changes are Markdown-only; no build or test commands are required. However, if any `.cs` source was touched, run:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/`\\n2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24167755634/agentic_workflow) · ● 434.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57967\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-09T01:40:34Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-09T01:40:34Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

Replace the current terse section with:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Extension Resources\\n\\nExtension resources have a flexible scope — they can be attached to any Azure resource. Their contextual path starts with a single `resourceUri` variable:\\n\\n```\\n/{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName}\\n```\\n\\nThe `resourceUri` parameter is treated as a contextual parameter derived from the parent resource\\u0026#39;s `Id`.\\n\\n#### Scope-Based Extension Resources\\n\\nSome extension resources also capture the parent resource **type** as a contextual parameter in addition to its name. For example, a `ChaosTarget` contextual path looks like:\\n\\n```\\n/{scope}/providers/Microsoft.Chaos/targets/{targetName}\\n````\\n\\nWhen the generator builds `ContextualParameter` objects for such resources, each variable segment in the path is paired with the constant key that precedes it using a LIFO (stack-based) algorithm. The variable pairs are pushed onto the stack in **key-then-value** order so that the resulting positional list matches the path order from outermost to innermost scope.\\n\\nThe generated REST call correctly supplies arguments in this order:\\n\\n````csharp\\n_restClient.Get(\\n    Id.Parent.ResourceType.Type,  // e.g., \\u0026#34;Microsoft.Kubernetes/connectedClusters\\u0026#34;\\n    Id.Parent.Name,               // e.g., \\u0026#34;myCluster\\u0026#34;\\n    Id.Name,                      // e.g., \\u0026#34;myTarget\\u0026#34;\\n    cancellationToken\\n);\\n```\\n\\nIf the stack push order were reversed (a previous bug), `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` would be swapped, producing incorrect REST calls.\\n````\\n\\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` (edit)\\n\\nAdd a new subsection under **Advanced Scenarios** documenting the emitter\\u0026#39;s `ValidateResourceId` generation for complex parent paths. Add after the **Singleton Resources** subsection:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### ValidateResourceId for Complex Multi-Provider Scopes\\n\\nWhen generating a resource collection, the emitter computes the **expected parent resource type** to emit a `ValidateResourceId` guard in the `GetAll` method. For simple paths (≤ 3 segments), the parent type is derived directly from the second-to-last provider segment.\\n\\nFor nested multi-provider paths with more than 3 segments — such as:\\n\\n```\\nmanagementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/quotas\\n```\\n\\nthe emitter falls back to computing the parent scope resource type from the **last `/providers/`** segment in the path. This ensures that `ValidateResourceId` is emitted correctly for these collection types even when the parent path has complex, multi-level scope nesting.\\n````\\n\\n#### File 3: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/CHANGELOG.md` (create)\\n\\nCreate a new CHANGELOG file to track generator/emitter changes:\\n\\n```markdown\\n# Changelog\\n\\n## Unreleased\\n\\n### Bug Fixes\\n\\n- Fixed incorrect parameter ordering for scope-based extension resources (e.g., `ChaosTarget`, `KubernetesClusterExtension`). The LIFO stack in `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy` was reversed, swapping `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` in generated REST client arguments.\\n- Fixed `ValidateResourceId` generation for resource collections whose parent paths contain more than 3 segments (nested multi-provider scopes such as `managementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/...`). The emitter now falls back to the last `/providers/` segment to compute the expected parent resource type.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo README snippet placeholders are needed for these documentation-only changes (the docs are in `.md` files under `docs/`, not in a package README that uses the snippet generation tooling).\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is an npm package README (not a client library README), so the standard section checklist (Getting Started, Key Concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, etc.) is not required. No changes needed to `README.md`.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nThe docs changes are Markdown-only; no build or test commands are required. However, if any `.cs` source was touched, run:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/`\\n2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24167755634/agentic_workflow) · ● 434.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57967\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-09T01:40:34Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-09T01:40:34Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-09T01:45:45.5062869Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T01:44:12.270332594Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":11,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4223768142,\"number\":57931,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Management Generator] Docs: Document extension resource detection with specific parent types\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n**Triggered by:** Commit [REDACTED] (PR #57722) by `@live1206`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57722 fixed a bug in the management generator's resource detection logic to correctly handle **extension resources that target a specific parent resource type** (e.g., a resource that can only extend `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines` rather than any arbitrary resource). The fix touched `resource-detection.ts`, `resource-metadata.ts`, and `resolve-arm-resources-converter.ts`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The existing `docs/OperationContext.md` covers extension resources only for the **generic/wildcard scope** case (paths starting with `/{resourceUri}/providers/...`), but does not explain extension resources that specify a **specific parent resource type** via the TypeSpec `@armResourceOperations` scope, nor how the generator detects and differentiates between the two\\n- There is no documentation explaining **how the generator detects whether an extension resource has a specific parent type vs. a wildcard scope**, and what generated code differs between the two cases (e.g., which extension class the resource's helper methods are generated on)\\n- No CHANGELOG file exists in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` to record this bug fix for users of the generator who may have been working around it\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` — **edit**\\n\\nExpand the existing **Extension Resources** subsection (under **Advanced Scenarios**) to cover the specific parent type case. Replace the current section:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Extension Resources\\n\\nExtension resources have a flexible scope (can extend any resource). Their contextual path starts with a single variable segment:\\n```\\n/{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName}\\n```\\n\\nThe system handles these specially, treating `resourceUri` as a contextual parameter derived from the parent resource's Id.\\n````\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Extension Resources\\n\\nExtension resources extend another Azure resource. The generator recognizes two sub-types:\\n\\n#### Wildcard-scope extension resources\\n\\nWhen an extension resource can extend **any** resource type, the operation paths use a single variable prefix:\\n\\n```\\n/{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName}\\n```\\n\\nThe system treats `resourceUri` as a contextual parameter derived from the extended resource's `Id`. Helper methods are added to the `ArmClient` mockable extension class so callers can invoke them from any `ArmResource`.\\n\\n#### Specific-parent extension resources\\n\\nWhen an extension resource is scoped to a **specific parent resource type** (for example, it can only extend `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`), the TypeSpec definition constrains the scope. The detected parent type determines which Azure scope class (e.g., `ResourceGroupResource`, `SubscriptionResource`, `TenantResource`, `ManagementGroupResource`, or the p
